2
The Ever-Evolving
Avatar 	WORKBOOK
MODULE 2
Exercise 1:
The “Hidden” Level:
Symptomatically Aware
This exercise is designed to help you take the “problems” your prospect may be aware of - and make it
symptomatic of a GREATER (but solvable) problem that sheds light on their situation, and renews hope
around a positive outcome.
Another way of approaching this is asking:
“What’s the problem within the problem”?
Percieved Problem
(Symptom)
REAL problem
(that you’re uniquely qualified to fix)
ie. poor conversions 	ie. wrong traffic sources/targeting
ie. difficulty adding muscle mass 	ie. ineffective training protocol for body type
ie. lack of productivity ie. not focused on specific, measurable,
and exciting goals.
ie. high employee turnover 	ie. poor leadership
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

3
The Ever-Evolving
Avatar 	WORKBOOK
MODULE 2
Exercise 2:
For each symptom, rewrite
the following sentence:
I know you’re experiencing _____ (perceived problem), but that’s actually symptomatic of something
deeper. The problem isn’t that _____(perceived problem), it’s actually that _____ (real problem), and
the good news is, it can be solved by _____(the result your program or product delivers).
EXAMPLE:
I know you’ve been having trouble getting your teenage daughter to be respectful, but that’s actually
symptomatic of something deeper. The problem isn’t that you don’t have the right set of communication
tools, it’s that you lack the underlying trust that even makes communication possible. The good news is,
trust can be repaired and rebuilt, even in the most hostile and damaged relationships.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
